content.text: MIDI & Sync
The EP-133 offers comprehensive connectivity options including MIDI, analog sync, and USB for integrating with other gear. OS 2.0 added MIDI Thru and support for up to 16 MIDI devices.
MIDI
The MIDI port uses a 3.5mm TRS connector (Type-A, MMA-compliant). Use a TRS-to-DIN adapter cable for standard 5-pin MIDI connections.
MIDI Output
The EP-133 sends MIDI notes when you play pads, allowing you to trigger external synthesizers and drum machines. MIDI clock is also sent to sync external gear to the EP-133's tempo.
MIDI Input
Receive MIDI notes to trigger EP-133 sounds from external controllers or sequencers. The EP-133 can also receive MIDI clock to sync to an external tempo source.
MIDI Thru
OS 2.0 added MIDI Thru functionality. Incoming MIDI messages are passed through to the output, allowing you to chain multiple MIDI devices through the EP-133.
MIDI Thru is configured in the System menu (SHIFT + ERASE). Enable or disable MIDI Thru as needed for your setup.
Sync
The analog SYNC port connects to Pocket Operators and other Teenage Engineering devices. It also supports Eurorack and vintage sync formats.
Sync Modes
Connecting Pocket Operators
Connect a Pocket Operator to the SYNC port using a standard 3.5mm stereo cable. The left channel carries audio, the right channel carries sync. Use PO-8 or PO-16 mode depending on your setup.

content.title: Multiple Pocket Operators
content.text: Use a splitter cable to connect two Pocket Operators simultaneously - select PO-16 mode for this configuration.
USB MIDI
The USB-C port provides class-compliant MIDI over USB. Connect to a computer, iPad, or other USB host to send/receive MIDI without additional interfaces.

content.caption: USB-C port for USB MIDI and power

content.text: MIDI Channel Configuration
Configure MIDI channels in the System menu (SHIFT + ERASE). You can set:
Syncing with a DAW

To sync the EP-133 with a DAW, connect via USB and configure your DAW to send MIDI clock. Set the EP-133's clock source to USB in the System menu. The EP-133 will follow your DAW's tempo and transport.
